title: Brothy Slow-Cooked Coconut Chicken & Rice
summary: Transform leftovers from our Slow-Roasted Curried Coconut Chicken into a golden, soupy rice bowl with fresh coriander, zingy lime and crispy onion on top. It’s pure comfort.

method.description: Make the Slow-Roasted Curried Coconut Chicken batch recipe.
Tip the chicken and coconut broth into a saucepan with the chicken stock. Set over a medium heat and warm through until piping hot.

Lift out the chicken pieces, if you’re using the breast, slice it up. If you’ve got leg meat, shred it with two forks. Taste the broth and season with salt, black pepper and a good squeeze of lime juice, if it needs it.
Reheat the rice and divide between bowls. Ladle over the hot broth, then top with the sliced or shredded chicken. Garnish with coriander leaves, crispy onions and serve with lime wedges on the side.

chefs.name: Chloe René
chefs.summary: Chloe (@chlo_eat) takes inspiration from her diverse upbringing to create recipes and dishes influenced from cultures around the world. Despite her classical training, Chloe's philosophy in the kitchen revolves around simplicity and home-cooking. She firmly believes that the essence of a great meal lies in the quality of ingredients and the care put into their preparation. Instead of relying on complex and technical processes, Chloe prizes the art of letting ingredients shine through, enhancing their natural flavours with a delicate touch.
